channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: U.S. Troops in Middle East Wait for the Next Big Moment
item.description: The U.S. military is essentially on standby as officials give contradictory signals about the status of the Iran war effort.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper and Eric Schmitt
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Soldiers of the 82nd Airborne Division at Fort Bragg, N.C., in 2021.

item.title: War and Energy Shortages Boost China’s Influence in Asia
item.description: The war in Iran has left China’s neighbors appealing for help, handing Beijing the kind of sway it has long sought.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Oil storage tanks at a depot in Hong Kong. Shipments of Chinese oil products to select countries have offered relief during the war and underscored how Beijing engenders good will in times of crisis, experts say.

item.title: Trump’s Indiana Victory Shows His Enduring Grip on Core Republican Supporters
item.description: Low approval ratings? MAGA divisions? The president was able to turn out party loyalists in an Indiana primary to help him oust Republican state lawmakers who had crossed him.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: Iran War Looms Over Vance as He Visits Iowa
item.description: The vice president, a skeptic of the war, made his first visit of the cycle to the state that will kick off the 2028 nominating process.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance visited Iowa on Tuesday and told an emotional story of meeting Gold Star families, including one that had lost a loved one in the war in Iran.

item.title: Tennessee Republicans Unveil New Congressional Map Carving Up Majority-Black House District
item.description: The General Assembly is expected to quickly approve the map, which slices up Memphis, a majority-Black city that makes up most of the state’s lone Democratic district.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane
item.media:credit: Brad J. Vest for The New York Times
item.media:description: From left, Stacey Abrams, a former Georgia state representative, U.S. Representative Steve Cohen, Tennessee State Senator Raumesh Akbari and Tennessee State Senator London Lamar meet after Republican legislators in Tennessee unveiled a new House map.

item.title: Susan Collins Says She Has Long Had a Benign Tremor
item.description: The Republican senator from Maine, running for re-election at age 73 in one of this year’s top Senate races, made the disclosure after mounting online scrutiny on the left.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.media:description: Senator Susan Collins of Maine, a Republican, is likely to face Graham Platner, a 41-year-old Democrat and a political newcomer, in the November general election.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Purported Suicide Note Is Released by Federal Judge
item.description: A federal judge released the note, which Jeffrey Epstein’s former cellmate said he found in a graphic novel. The New York Times has not authenticated that Mr. Epstein wrote it.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Weiser, Jan Ransom and Steve Eder

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the Last Televised California Governor Debate
item.description: Candidates debated housing and insurance policy in the first half, then furiously attacked one another at the end.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla and Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:description: Seven candidates competing to be the next governor of California met for a debate at the Skirball Cultural Center in Los Angeles on Wednesday.

item.title: Here’s what to know about the California governor race.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:description: Antonio Villaraigosa, the former mayor of Los Angeles; Katie Porter, a former state representative; and the businessman Tom Steyer are among the candidates in a crowded field vying to become the next governor of California.

item.title: The Longevity Secrets Helping Athletes Blow Past the Limits of Age
item.description: With cutting-edge sports medicine and sci-fi gadgetry, more and more athletes are figuring out how to extend their careers.
item.dc:creator: Devin Gordon and Dina Litovsky

item.title: Starmer Braces for Local Election Losses Amid New Era of UK Politics
item.description: Polls predict historic losses for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s Labour Party on Thursday as anti-immigrant Reform U.K. makes gains, and a new era of multiparty politics takes shape.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear and Stephen Castle
item.media:credit: Andrew Testa for The New York Times
item.media:description: A mural showing Aneurin Bevan, the Labour Party hero who helped create the U.K.’s National Health Service, in Tredegar, Wales.

item.title: Starmer Faces a Major Test in a Set of UK Elections: What to Know
item.description: Local elections in England, and parliamentary elections in Scotland and Wales, take place on Thursday. The governing Labour Party is expected to do very badly.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle
item.media:credit: Stefan Rousseau/Press Association, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain at the launch of the Labour Party’s local election campaign, in Wolverhampton, England, in March.

item.title: The ‘Perfect Storm’ Hanging Over Britain’s Public Debt
item.description: Bond investors are expected to scrutinize the results of critical local elections on Thursday as the war in the Middle East adds inflation pressure on the government.
item.dc:creator: Eshe Nelson
item.media:credit: Joseph Horton for The New York Times
item.media:description: Investors are betting the Bank of England will approve several interest rate increases this year, reversing previous expectations that rates would come down.

item.title: How the Fight Over Israel Is Playing Out Inside MAGA
item.description: The war in Iran has added to a tectonic shift in public opinion — a bipartisan swing away from Israel. Some on the far-right are fighting to keep President Trump’s movement aligned with the Jewish state.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Annie Flanagan for The New York Times
item.media:description: Laura Loomer, the far-right media figure, has emerged as one of the president’s most aggressive, pro-Israel enforcers.

item.title: Ted Turner, Creator of CNN and the 24-Hour News Cycle, Dies at 87
item.description: As one of the most important figures in media history, he oversaw a vast cable empire of news, sports and entertainment channels.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Kandell
item.media:description: Ted Turner was a visionary media mogul who dominated the cable television industry, and extended his reach into movies, major league baseball and even yachting.

item.title: Giuliani Seeks to Have Health Care Covered by 9/11 Program
item.description: The former New York City mayor, who is improving after a serious case of pneumonia, suffers from a respiratory condition linked to his exposure to toxins at ground zero, his spokesman says.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Goldstein and Jonah E. Bromwich
item.media:description: Rudolph W. Giuliani, the former New York City mayor, attended the ceremony commemorating the 24th anniversary of the Sept. 11, 2001, terrorist attacks.

item.title: Judge Says F.B.I. Can Keep 2020 Election Records Seized From Georgia
item.description: The ruling is the latest in a saga driven by President Trump’s desire for redemption in the state, which he lost in 2020. The county is likely to appeal.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk and Richard Fausset
item.media:credit: Nicole Craine for The New York Times
item.media:description: The F.B.I. searched an election center in Fulton County, Ga., in January.

item.title: Howard Lutnick Grilled by Lawmakers Over Epstein Ties
item.description: The commerce secretary appeared for hours in a closed-door session on Wednesday with the House Oversight Committee
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold and Ana Swanson
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick on Capitol Hill in April. He is one of the highest-profile cabinet members to come under scrutiny in connection with Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Jack Smith Calls the Justice Dept. ‘Corrupted’ by Trump and His Allies
item.description: At a private event in Washington last month, Mr. Smith, the former special counsel, accused Justice Department leaders of targeting people for prosecution to please and impress the president.
item.dc:creator: Glenn Thrush
item.category: Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (Documents Case)
Federal Criminal Case Against Trump (2020 Election Case)
item.media:description: Jack Smith, the special counsel who twice indicted President Trump, testifying at a hearing in January in Washington.

item.title: Hantavirus Patients Land in Amsterdam With More Cruise Ship Evacuations Planned
item.description: Three people with possible symptoms of the disease were evacuated on Wednesday morning from the MV Hondius before it headed to the Canary Islands.
item.dc:creator: Claire Moses, Aimee Ortiz and Rylee Kirk
item.media:description: The MV Hondius off the port of Praia, the capital of Cape Verde, on Wednesday.

item.title: Health Authorities Monitor Hantavirus Cruise Passengers in United States
item.description: People in at least three states are being monitored after being on the ship. None have shown symptoms, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk and Nina Agrawal
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:description: Three sick people aboard the MV Hondius were evacuated on Wednesday.

item.title: Should You Worry About Hantavirus?
item.description: Here’s what to know about the virus, how it spreads and the risk to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Nina Agrawal and Dani Blum

item.title: A Landslide in Alaska Set Off a Tsunami. There May Be More to Come.
item.description: Scientists say as glaciers retreat in a warming climate, landslide-generated tsunamis are likely to become more frequent.
item.dc:creator: Christian Elliott

item.title: Air-Conditioning Is in Short Supply as Asia Swelters
item.description: From classrooms to crowded buses, people across South and Southeast Asia are adjusting to life with less cooling — or none at all — as the war in Iran drives an energy crunch.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado, Muktita Suhartono and Saif Hasnat
item.category: Far East, South and Southeast Asia and Pacific Areas
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Used air-conditioners for sale at a roadside shop in Manila, Philippines, in April.

item.title: New Study Shows Risks of Amazon Deforestation. And Rewards of Protection.
item.description: Researchers examined the combined effects of tree loss and global warming in an effort to better understand how and when an ecosystem collapse could unfold.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey
item.media:description: A timber truck rolled through a deforested section of the Amazon in Pará State, Brazil, in November.

item.title: Trump Keeps Saying He Wants to Leave NATO. Maybe He Already Has.
item.description: For the NATO deterrent to work, Europe and Russia both have to believe that America is coming.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Major

item.title: The New El Niño Might Make 2027 the Hottest Year on Record
item.description: The El Niño brewing this year might make 2027 the hottest year on record.
item.dc:creator: David Wallace-Wells

item.title: Late Night Isn’t Buying Trump’s New ‘Skirmish’ in Iran
item.description: After Trump used the terms “miniwar” and “excursion” to describe the conflict in Iran, Jimmy Kimmel predicted that the president would next call the war “a tiff. It will be a $200 billion tiff.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: The Boxing Match That Killed a Police Recruit
item.description: A Massachusetts State Police cadet, already hobbled from a previous bout, raised his hand to take on a more skilled fighter, and investigators say no one stopped him. Four people face charges.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker and Aimee Ortiz
item.media:description: Jennifer Penton of the Massachusetts State Police, left, and Troopers David Montanez, center, and Edwin Rodriguez were arraigned on charges of involuntary manslaughter in the death of Enrique Delgado-Garcia last month.

item.title: Meditating or Rebooting? A Robot Buddhist Monk Comes to Korea.
item.description: Gabi, the newest monk at a temple in Seoul, vowed to not overcharge as it seeks enlightenment.
item.dc:creator: Remy Tumin
item.media:credit: Yonhap News Agency, via via Reuters

item.title: Elon Musk’s Confidante Shivon Zilis Is Cast as His Inside Source at OpenAI
item.description: Shivon Zilis worked closely with Elon Musk while she was on OpenAI’s board. Her ties to the world’s richest man were detailed in a landmark trial on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Brennan Smart for The New York Times
item.media:description: Shivon Zilis, a longtime confidante of Elon Musk and the mother of four of his children, testified in a federal trial on Wednesday.

item.title: Anthropic’s C.E.O. Says It Could Grow by 80 Times This Year
item.description: The chief executive, Dario Amodei, said the rapid growth had exponentially increased the start-up’s need for more computing power.
item.dc:creator: Natallie Rocha
item.media:description: Anthropic’s chief executive, Dario Amodei, in January. On Wednesday, he spoke at his company’s annual developer conference.
